title: Eddie Brock
content:
{{Otheruses|Lethal Protector}} {{MarvelLegacy|Sony|[[Lethal Protector Venom Universe]]|4=Characters|6=''Venom'', ''Venom: Let There Be Carnage'', or ''Venom: The Last Dance''}} {{Character |image = Eddie Brock (NWH).png |real name = {{Conjecture|Edward Charles Allan}} Brock |alias = {{Alias |primary = Eddie Brock{{Ref|SMNWH}} |codenames = Lethal Protector{{Ref|SMNWH}}}} |species = [[Human]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |gender = Male |movie = ''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]'' ([[List of Post-credits Scenes|mid-credits scene]]) |actor = [[Tom Hardy]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|Señor, [[Thanos|he]] made my family [[Snap|disappear]]. For five years."
"Five years? That's a long time. I mean, maybe I should go to [[New York City|New York]] and speak to this... [[Spider-Man]].|[[List of Minor Characters/Phase Four#Spider-Man: No Way Home|Bartender]] and Eddie Brock|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} '''{{Conjecture|Edward Charles Allan}} "Eddie" Brock''' is the [[human]] host of the [[symbiote]] [[Venom]]. Together, the pair are known as '''Lethal Protector'''. After being transported to another universe as a result of a [[Runes of Kof-Kol|spell]] cast by [[Doctor Strange]], he learned about the [[Avengers]], [[Spider-Man]] and [[Thanos]] before being returned back to his universe. ==Biography== {{MD|Edward Brock (Earth-688B)||1}} ===Sudden Arrival at a New Universe=== {{Quote|Where are we? What did you do?"
"It wasn't me.|Eddie Brock and [[Venom]]|wikipedia:Venom: Let There Be Carnage}} Eddie Brock was bonded to the [[symbiote]] known as [[Venom]] and together they became the vigilante known as the Lethal Protector. While in [[Mexico]], Brock returned to his hotel room and laid down on the bed,{{Ref|VLTBC}} unbeknownst to Brock, in another universe, a [[Runes of Kof-Kol|spell]] cast by [[Doctor Strange]] transported people from any universe who knew [[Peter Parker]]'s secret identity as Spider-Man into his own.{{Ref|SMNWH}} As a result of Venom accessing a [[Multiverse|multiversal]] symbiote hive mind,{{Ref|VLTBC}} Venom became aware of Parker being Spider-Man and thus was taken out of its universe, and brought into Strange's universe, along with its host Brock.{{Ref|SMNWH}} Finding themselves in a resort room different from theirs, Brock quickly sat up, and questioned his surroundings before turning on the television. Venom and Brock saw a report from ''[[The Daily Bugle]]'' where [[J. Jonah Jameson]] showed a photo of Parker and harshly criticized Spider-Man for his actions. Upon seeing Parker's face on the television, Venom took over Brock's body and licked the television.{{Ref|VLTBC}}[https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-features/venom-2-post-credits-scene-mcu-carnage-1235024589/ Venom 2 Post Credits Scene and the Future of MCU – The Hollywood Reporter][https://collider.com/is-venom-in-mcu-kevin-feige-comments/ Kevin Feige Discusses the Decision to Put Venom in the MCU] ===Returning to His Universe=== [[File:Eddie Brock (Spider-Man No Way Home).png|{{AltFloat}}|Brock learns of the history of the universe]] {{Quote|Now, tell me again about your [[Thanos|purple alien]] that loves [[Infinity Stones|stones]]. 'Cause I'll tell you what, man, aliens, they do not love stones."
"Eddie! Don't start!"
"No, they do not love stones. Do you know what aliens love? Eating brains! Because that's what they do.|Eddie Brock and [[Venom]]|Spider-Man: No Way Home}} Brock then left the room and went to the resort's bar in an attempt to make sense of his surroundings. He conversed with a bartender and learned that he was in another universe where several individuals with superhuman abilities existed. Brock was told of [[Iron Man]] and [[Hulk]]'s existence, as well as how an alien named [[Thanos]] sought to [[Infinity War|obtain]] the [[Infinity Stones]] and caused people to [[Snap|disappear]]. Brock dismissed the story as he believed all aliens had the singular desire to eat brains as Venom did. The bartender took offense at this, informing Brock that Thanos caused his family to disappear for five years. [[File:Eddie Brock returns to his universe.png|{{AltFloat}}|Brock returns to his home universe]] Brock took sympathy on the bartender and expressed a desire to go to [[New York City]] and meet with [[Spider-Man]]. Brock stood up to leave while Venom suggested that they go skinny dipping instead. As Brock informed Venom that they would not go skinny dipping, the bartender reminded him that he needed to pay the bill. However, Brock was swiftly transported back to his own universe as a result of [[Doctor Strange]]'s spell.{{Ref|SMNWH}} ==Relationships== ===Allies=== *[[Venom]] - Symbiote ==Gallery== Eddie Brock.png Eddie Brock (Spider-Man No Way Home).png Eddie Brock (2024).png Eddie Brock (No Way Home).png Eddie Brock returns to his universe.png ==Trivia== *In the comics, Eddie Brock is a reporter who was embarrassed in front of his fellow journalists by [[Spider-Man]]. Developing a hatred for Spider-Man, he headed to a church to commit suicide where he bonded with a [[Symbiotes|symbiote]], becoming [[Venom]]. *In {{WPS|Sony's Spider-Man Universe}}, Eddie Brock is a former journalist who became the host of a [[Symbiotes|symbiote]] named [[Venom]]. Brock helped Venom defeat other symbiotes like Riot. Brock and Venom became very close, which caused relationship problems between them as they battled [[Carnage]] and {{WPS|Shriek (character)|Shriek}}. ==Behind the Scenes== *Eddie Brock in the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] continues the live-action incarnation of the character from {{WPS|Sony's Spider-Man Universe}}, who was also portrayed by [[Tom Hardy]]; he is the second incarnation of the character, following an incarnation portrayed by {{WPS|Topher Grace}}. *[[Tom Hardy]] was uncredited for his appearance in ''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]''. *Eddie Brock and [[Venom]] are shown entering the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] in the mid-credits-scene of ''{{WPS|Venom: Let There Be Carnage}}''.{{Ref|VLTBC}} The scene was created through a collaboration between [[Marvel Studios]] and [[Sony Pictures Entertainment]][https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/kevin-feige-eternals-venom-spider-man-1235033550/ Kevin Feige on Eternals, Venom/Spider-Man Post-Credits Scene – The Hollywood Reporter] and was shot on the set of ''[[Spider-Man: No Way Home]]''.[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MDlSY7rDAfk 'Spider-Man: No Way Home' Interviews With Tom Holland, Zendaya, Kevin Feige & More - YouTube] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{WP}} *{{Marvel}} *{{Marvel|Edward Brock (Earth-688B)|Eddie Brock (Earth-688B)}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: No Way Home Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Brown Hair]] [[Category:Blue Eyes]]
